name: ScrollArea
description: A scrollable region with custom-styled scrollbars overlaid on native scroll. Native scrolling, keyboard navigation, touch gestures, and RTL are preserved; only the scrollbar visuals are replaced. Scrollbars fade in on hover/scroll and respect prefers-reduced-motion. Built on Base UI ScrollArea with StyleX styling.

Root.description: Outer container that establishes the scroll context. Positions Scrollbar and Corner absolutely over the viewport. Provides overflow state to children via data attributes.

overflowEdgeThreshold.type: number | { xStart?: number; xEnd?: number; yStart?: number; yEnd?: number }
overflowEdgeThreshold.description: Pixel threshold before overflow-edge data attributes are applied. Useful for offsetting scroll indicators.

dataAttributes.data-scrolling: Present while the user is scrolling.
dataAttributes.data-has-overflow-x: Present when content is wider than the viewport.
dataAttributes.data-has-overflow-y: Present when content is taller than the viewport.
dataAttributes.data-overflow-x-start: Present when there is overflow on the inline-start side.
dataAttributes.data-overflow-x-end: Present when there is overflow on the inline-end side.
dataAttributes.data-overflow-y-start: Present when there is overflow on the block-start side.
dataAttributes.data-overflow-y-end: Present when there is overflow on the block-end side.

Viewport.description: The actual scrollable container. Native scrollbars are visually hidden — Base UI's overlay scrollbars sit on top. Keyboard scrolling, RTL, and touch gestures use the native viewport.

Scrollbar.description: A vertical or horizontal scrollbar track. Hidden by default; fades in on hover or while scrolling. Supports drag-to-scroll on the thumb.

orientation.description: Whether the scrollbar controls vertical or horizontal scroll.

keepMounted.description: Keep in the DOM when the viewport isn't scrollable.

dataAttributes.data-hovering: Present when the pointer is over the scroll area.
dataAttributes.data-scrolling: Present while the user is scrolling.
dataAttributes.data-has-overflow-x: Present when content is wider than the viewport.
dataAttributes.data-has-overflow-y: Present when content is taller than the viewport.

Thumb.description: The draggable indicator showing scroll position and progress. Sized automatically to the visible-to-total content ratio.

Corner.description: Small square that fills the intersection of horizontal and vertical scrollbars. Renders only when both scrollbars are present.

cssRequirements.description: Scrollbar fade in/out is driven by data-hovering and data-scrolling on the Scrollbar part. StyleX cannot target data-attribute selectors, so this rule must live in a global stylesheet inside @layer priority1. Honors prefers-reduced-motion.

intents.reasoning: ScrollArea overlays a custom-styled scrollbar on top of native scroll, keeping all native behaviors (keyboard, touch, RTL, mouse wheel) while making the scrollbar match the design system.
ScrollArea with a horizontal Scrollbar gives a clean horizontal scrolling row (chips, tags, thumbnails) with a styled scrollbar that fades in only when needed.
Inside a Dialog or Drawer, ScrollArea provides consistent scrollbar styling that won't clash with the surrounding chrome and stays out of the way until needed.

avoidWhen.scenario: The whole document or viewport scrolls
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use the browser's native scroll for full-page scrolling. ScrollArea is for a bounded region; using it on the document hijacks page scroll and breaks anchor links and accessibility heuristics.

avoidWhen.scenario: Virtualized lists with thousands of items
avoidWhen.reasoning: ScrollArea wraps native scroll but doesn't virtualize. For very large lists, pair it with a virtualization library (TanStack Virtual, react-window) or skip it entirely — the custom scrollbar may interfere with item measurement.

avoidWhen.scenario: Content that should grow with the page
avoidWhen.reasoning: ScrollArea is for fixed-size regions. If the container should expand to fit content, just use a regular div — no scrollbar is needed.

examples.name: basic
examples.description: Vertical scroll with a styled scrollbar

examples.name: horizontal
